L36 Multiple Conductor Termination: Multiple conductors may be secured to a single termination point. There is no limit to the number of conductors that may be secured to a single terminal, provided that all requirements specified in 29.3.7 are met.

L37 Flexible Bus Bar: Flexible bus bar is treated as wire or a flat conductor (Listed or Recognized AWM). There is no SCCR required. Flexible bus bar shall be routed in accordance with 29.4. Flexible bus bar is covered by Recognitions under QEUY2 and optionally covered under AVLV2. Recognitions under AVLV2 do not require Procedure description. Recognitions under QEUY2 require Procedure description. The ampacity of busbars Recognized under AVLV2 shall be determined by using Table 28.1 and comparing their cross-sectional area to the equivalent conductor size in the table. Alternatively, their ampacity may be determined based on their current density not exceeding 1000 amperes per square inch (per 6.45 cm2) of cross-sectional area (minimum width of bus bar multiplied by minimum thickness of bus bar) of the copper bus bar. Recognized bus bar systems, including flexible bus bars covered under QEUY2, shall have all construction details described in the Procedure.

L38 Disconnecting Means: A disconnecting means is not required to be part of an industrial control panel and may be provided in an industrial control panel with or without branch circuit protection, see 30.3.1. A disconnecting means provided as a part of an industrial control panel shall comply with Section 30.

L39 Combination Motor Controllers: In lieu of specific Procedure description noted in Table 1 of ?UL 508A Supplement SA Specific Component Requirements?, combination motor controllers can be constructed using the specific manufacturer and part number of a Recognized instantaneous trip circuit breaker, motor controller, overload relay and any other associated components and combination ratings described on the UL website, see L58.

L40 Branch Circuit Protection: Branch circuit protection is not required to be a part of an industrial control panel, and branch circuit type fuses are not required to be installed in rejection type fuseholders provided in a panel, see 31.2.1. Branch circuit protection or rejection type fuseholders provided as part of an industrial control panel shall comply with the requirements in Section 31 that apply for each of the intended loads, i.e. motor, receptacle, heater, appliance, or lighting, to be supplied from the panel. Starting from the load side of the panel, the branch circuit protection is the first component that complies with 31.1.1 ? 31.1.6. Any of the components noted in 31.1.7 shall be ignored with respect to determining compliance with Section 31.

L41 For Future Use:

L42 Branch Circuit Protection of Motor Groups: The branch circuit protection for a motor shall be verified to comply with the requirements for a motor group, 31.4, when the branch circuit protective device carries the current of more than one load. The tap conductors shall be sized to have an ampacity that is not less than 1/3 the ampacity of the branch circuit conductor, that carries the combined load current of the motor group, in accordance with 31.4.3. Non-motor loads connected to a motor group shall be provided with an additional branch circuit protective device that complies with 31.4.4, 31.5 ? 31.8, unless the branch circuit protection for the motor group meets these requirements.

Paragraph | Test Name | Additional Notes
79.1 | Ground Fault Protection Test | a) Required only when the control panel is additionally marked ?Suitable for use as service equipment?

b) The results of tests performed to Section 79 shall be included with installation instructions as described in 78.1(e).
B3.2 | Ground Fault Circuit Interrupter Functional Test | a) Required only when a component not previously investigated by UL is installed in accordance with Appendix B.

b) The test records of tests performed to Section B3 shall be complete, as described in B3.3.
